subject: Grow Your XRP Holdings
body:
Salutations, crypto enthusiasts!
We are delighted to be announcing the launch of our Accelerator Program.
With regard to recent events in the decentralized ecosystem, it has become increasingly important to take ownership and custody of your digital assets.
Our program is designed to incentivize users to hold and stake their XRP while increasing awareness of private key ownership.
To participate, simply whitelist your account before the campaign ends. Our Token Allocation Tool will analyze and estimate your rewards based on account metrics such as age, transactional activity, trading, tokenization, and NFT involvement.
For recent holders, there will be a maximum reward multiplier of 23.5% based on their contributing XRP balance, and existing holders can obtain an increase of up to 33.9% with the five possible multipliers.
For further details, take the time to read our most recent blog post.

Stake XRP

Best regards,The Ripple Executive Team

subject: [UAI] 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems
body: FINAL Call for papers - 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications (AISTA2004) **** Please apologize for multiple copies **** FINAL Call for Papers AISTA 2004 --> 2004 IEEE International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications November 15-18, 2004 --> Centre de Recherche Public Henri Tudor, Luxembourg-Kirchberg, Luxembourg http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004 IMPORTANT DATES: Submission of extended summaries: September 15, 2004 Notification of acceptance: September 26, 2004 Camera-ready paper due: October 24, 2004 AISTA Conference: November 15-18, 2004 AIMS and SCOPE: The International Conference on Advances in Intelligent Systems - Theory and Applications - AISTA'2004 aims at bringing together theoreticians and practitioners of Intelligent Systems from Luxembourg and around the world to exchange ideas, present their recent results and discuss possible collaborations. The focus of the conference includes, but is not limited=20 to the areas listed below. * Control Systems: Neural Networks Control, Fuzzy Logic Control, Genetic Algorithms & Evolutionary Control, Model-Predictive Control, Adaptive and Optimal Control, Intelligent Control Systems, Robotics and Automation, Fault Diagnosis, Intelligent agents & Multiagent Systems, Industrial Automations Hybrid Systems; Fuzzy Evolutionary Systems, Fuzzy Expert Systems, Fuzzy Neural Systems, Neural Genetic Systems, Neural-Fuzzy-Genetic Systems, Hybrid Systems for Optimization. * Optimization and Prediction: Multi-criteria Optimization, Signal Processing, Time Series Analysis & Prediction, System Identification, Data Mining & Knowledge Discovery, Intelligent Information Systems, Image Processing, Parallel Computing applications in Identification & Control, Pattern Recognition, Clustering, Classification. * Decision Making: Intelligent Databases & Information Retrieval, Case-Based Reasoning, Decision Analysis, Dynamic Systems Modeling, Decision Support Systems, Qualitative and Approximate-Reasoning Modeling. * Security: Intrusion Detection, Privilege Determining, Denial-of-Service Resistance, Attack Resistance, Cryptographic Mechanisms and Protocols, Mobile Environment Security, Wireless Security, Access control, Database=20 Security, Identification and Authentication. * Workshop: A workshop dedicated to Satellite Control and Communications will take place in parallel to the conference sessions. Topics might come for=20 example from the field of Intelligent Ground Control Systems, Autonomous Satellite Cluster Control, Intelligent Telemetry Processing, and Intelligent Antenna Systems. SUBMISSION OF PAPERS Papers reporting original and unpublished research or application results pertaining to the above and related topics are solicited. Papers will be reviewed on the basis of two pages extended abstracts of sufficient detail to permit reasonable evaluation. Accepted papers will appear in the conference proceedings with an ISBN. The final version of accepted papers should be of six pages long. Submissions should include the title, author(s), affiliation(s), e-mail address(es), tel/fax numbers, postal address(es) and keywords on the first page. Papers should be submitted on the conference web site: http://aista.tudor.lu/aista2004. If web=20 submission is not possible, manuscripts should be sent as an attachment via email to one of the Program Chairs listed below on before the deadline date of September 5, 2004. A special issue of the international of Integrated=20 Computer-Aided Engineering (ICAE) will be devoted to publish extended versions of the=20 best papers=20 presented at AISTA'04. subject: My (Simple) Summer To Do List
body: My (Simple) Summer To Do List The five projects I plan to tackle ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ the newsletterBlog InstagramSHOPtiktokI don't know how things go in your house, but in our home summer is a time to slooooooow down. The kids are home and creating chaos, I have a lot less time to get work done, and time just seems to move a little differently. I wouldn't have it any other way - I love to soak up every minute I can of their time off! But, it often means that things on the ol' to-do list don't get crossed of quite as quickly or efficiently. Since I always function better when I write things down, I sat down this week to write out a quick list of 5 things I want to do around the house this summer. Just five! It's low pressure, simple, and doable (hopefully). And if I can cross all of these things off the list while also having fun with the kids, I can call it a wildly successful summer. Here's my summer project list. (I'd love to know what's on yours if you decide to make one, too!) Organize the craft closet. It's fallen into disarray after my big organization efforts last year and it's time. It's so bad I have refused to even go near it over the last couple of weeks, which is starting to become a problem, since it's next to the laundry room. 😂😬Build some window boxes. I've been toying with the idea of adding window boxes to the kids' play camper for quite a while now, and I think this summer is the time! It'll be a fun project to do with the kids, and adding some things to the camper always gets them freshly excited to be out there more.Spruce up the front porch. I'm great about decorating our front porch in the fall and around Christmastime. But other than that? I mostly ignore it. 😅 I have an idea I'm working on for something really fun to add that will make a big impact, and I want to knock it out this summer. It'll involve plants (of course!), and the vision is still coming together, but I'm excited.Do some deep cleaning. When we're home and things are moving a little slower, I find it's a great time to do some deeper cleaning projects. I'm setting a goal of deep cleaning one room or space each week - it obviously won't stay clean forever, but the efforts add up!Enjoy every minute. Listen, I can't be the only one who occasionally has to add things like “have some fun” on my to do list. Sometimes I get so caught up in trying to get all of my tasks done that I forget to just slow down and soak it up. So, the fifth (and perhaps most important) thing I'm adding to my to do list this summer is to not forget to have fun. I'm committing to one day “off” work each week to spend with the kids, and I've built intentional kid-focused time into my daily schedule for the summer.This summer is going to be light on the projects, but heavy on the fun. I can't wait to share it with you! What's on your to do list this summer? Would you be interested in hearing more about how we're having fun as a family, too?! Links of the weekI updated my “things that are bringing me joy this year" list - it's a good one! I went to Target this week and all of the cute summer stuff made me want to throw and outdoor party so badly. Can't stop thinking about the cutest outdoor plates, drink dispensers, and other serving accessories. Someone get me a backyard dining table, stat!I've been working on a big ol' list of all the things we love to use to keep us busy in these long summer days. If you need inspiration, you can see it here! See you next week! PO Box 2136Leander, TX 78646, USA Unsubscribe or Manage Preferences here. No hard feelings!

So far, I've restarted the servers and reviewed the firewall settings, but the issue still exists. I would be grateful if you could address this quickly and give me a solution soon. The data feed is crucial for our operations, and any further delays could have significant repercussions. Please let me know if you need more information from me to resolve this issue. You can contact me via this email or by phone. I appreciate your swift attention to this matter and look forward to your response soon.

subject: Account Info Verification ID Number: PP-065-617-349
body: Account Info Verification Dear FCU holder account,As part of our security measures, we regularly screen activity in Federal Credit Unions (FCU) network.We recently noticed the following issue on your account: A recent review of your account determined that we require some additional information from you in order to provide you with secure service. Case ID Number: PP-065-617-349 For your protection, we have limited access to your account until additional security measures can be completed. We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause. Please log in to your FCU account to restore your access as soon as possible. You must click the link below and fill in the form on the following page to complete the verification process. Click here to update your accountIn accordance with NCUA User Agreement, your account access will remain limited until the issue has been resolved. Unfortunately, if access to your account remains limited for an extended period of time, it may result in further limitations or eventual account closure. We encourage you to log in to your FCU account as soon as possible to help avoid this. We thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. Please understand that this is a security measure intended to help protect you and your account. We apologize for any inconvenience. Sincerely, NCUA Account Review Department

subject: Addressing an Emergent Quantum Threat
body:
Addressing an Emergent Quantum Threat

Dear Ledger User,

We are contacting you today regarding a critical development in digital asset security that requires your immediate attention. Our research and threat intelligence teams have identified a paradigm shift in potential attack vectors, moving from theoretical concepts to practical threats.

Specifically, we have found credible evidence of malicious actors leveraging emergent quantum computational power. These attacks are designed to exploit the underlying cryptographic principles—namely the Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm (ECDSA)—that secure most digital wallets. In essence, this advanced capability could allow sophisticated actors to derive a private key from a public address, an action previously considered computationally impossible.

We believe this new threat vector is responsible for a series of recent, unexplained fund thefts from various wallets across the ecosystem. To be clear: this is not a vulnerability in Ledger's hardware design, but an industry-wide challenge to the very foundation of current cryptographic standards.

Post-Quantum Firmware Update

To safeguard your holdings against this threat, we have released a mandatory firmware update. This is not a routine patch; it is a proactive cryptographic upgrade that integrates post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) countermeasures into your device's core architecture. This will fortify your hardware wallet against both current and future quantum attacks.

It is imperative that you install this update immediately to ensure the continued integrity of your assets. To update:

Ledger has always been at the forefront of digital asset security. Our mission requires us to anticipate and neutralize threats before they achieve widespread impact. This firmware update is a testament to that commitment.

Update Firmware via Ledger Live

Thank you for your immediate cooperation.

Sincerely,
The Ledger Cryptography & Security Team

Ledger SAS, 1 Rue du Mail, 75002 Paris, France

This is a critical security notification. Always verify the authenticity of such requests by launching Ledger Live directly and never from a link in an email you do not trust.

subject: Re: [ILUG] Formatting a windows partition from Linux
body: Update on this for anyone that's interested, and because I like closed threads... nothing worse than an infinite while loop, is there? I ended up formatting a floppy on my flatmate's (un-networked) P100 running FAT16 Win95, and mcopied the contents of the bootdisk across. Now I have a FAT16 Win98 install running alongside Slackware, and can play Metal Gear Solid when the mood takes me ;) /Ciaran. On Wednesday 21 August 2002 16:21, Ciaran Johnston wrote: > Dublin said: > > If you copy the files from your disk to the c: partition and mark it as > > active it should work ... > > Yeah, I figured that, but it doesn't seem to ... well, if that's the case > I'll give it another go tonight, maybe come back with some error messages. > > Just to clarify for those who didn't understand me initially - I have a > floppy drive installed, but it doesn't physically work. There's nowhere > handy to pick one up where I am, and I don't fancy waiting a few days for > one to arrive from Peats. > > Thanks for the answers, > Ciaran. > > > You especially need io.sys, command.com and msdos.sys > > > > your cd driver .sys and read the autoexec.bat and config.sys files for > > hints on what you did with your boot floppy > > > > P > > > > On Wed, 2002-08-21 at 14:07, Ciaran Johnston wrote: > >> Hi folks, > >> The situation is this: at home, I have a PC with 2 10Gig HDDs, and no > >> (working) floppy drive. I have been running Linux solely for the last > >> year, but recently got the urge to, among other things, play some of > >> my Windoze games. I normally install the windows partition using a > >> boot floppy which I have conveniently zipped up, but I haven't any way > >> of writing or reading a floppy. > >> So, how do I go about: > >> 1. formatting a C: drive with system files (normally I would use > >> format /s c: from the floppy). > >> 2. Installing the CDROM drivers (my bootdisk (I wrote it many years > >> ago) does this normally). > >> 3. Booting from the partition? > >> > >> I wiped all my linux partitions from the first drive and created > >> partitions for Windows (HDA1) Slackware and RedHat. I used cfdisk for > >> this. I made the first drive (hda) bootable. I then installed the > >> windows partition in LILO and reran lilo (installed in MBR). I copied > >> the contents of boot.zip to my new windows partition and tried to boot > >> it - all I get is a garbled line of squiggles. > >> > >> Anyone any ideas? I can't think of anywhere in Athlone to get a new > >> floppy drive this evening... > >> > >> Thanks, > >> Ciaran. > >> > >> > >> > >> -- > >> Irish Linux Users' Group: ilug@linux.ie > >> http://www.linux.ie/mailman/listinfo/ilug for (un)subscription > >> information. subject: Bridging the gap between Liberty and Microsoft (Tech Update)
body:
Tech Update Today

VITAL SIGNS FOR JULY 15, 2002

David Berlind
Bridging the gap between Liberty and Microsoft
It appears that we are closer to a solution for single sign-on and identity management. But what if Microsoft chooses not to support Liberty's specifications?
Let's look at the worst-case scenario

White House reviews security backbone

Breaking the wireless speed barrier?

Pentium 4 to power Dell budget PCs

AMD chips at mobile, flash markets

Norway cancels Microsoft contract

Hackers challenge Internet monitoring

More Enterprise News

Farber's Picks
Dan
Farber Microsoft stresses security for Exchange
Microsoft is readying a new Exchange Server e-mail system, with improved security, a facelift for Outlook, and better support for cell phones and other handhelds. The upcoming version, the company's first major update of the product since releasing Exchange 2000 nearly two years ago, will by default disable certain messaging features to prevent hackers and virus makers from taking advantage of openings. While Microsoft's biggest nemesis in the e-mail and messaging market is Lotus, the company also competes against Novell and newcomer Oracle, which plans to release its own e-mail and messaging software later this year.
Read the full story IBM puts more bite in 'Shark'
Can IBM's release of a new top-end "Shark" enterprise storage system boost the company's prospects in the depressed storage market? Big Blue has fixed many early Shark problems, improving everything from data transfer capabilities to cache size, and adding RAID 10 support and Eliza self-healing features. But IBM faces the still dominant EMC, which last week lured away Mark Lewis from HP to become its new CTO; and Hitachi, which still boasts the technology edge. IBM's edge is its ability to bundle Shark sales with related server and service deals.
Read the full story Report: Linux hack attacks on the rise
Hackers are increasingly targeting Linux-based Web servers, while the number of successful attacks on Windows systems has decreased, according to a new report. Linux server applications are often vulnerable to attack because of mismanagement, according to the study, which urges Linux system administrators to be more vigilant about patching known security bugs.
Read the full story Frethem worm variant: "Re: Your password!"
An e-mail message that promises to reveal secret information with a password is a variant from the pesky Frethem worm family. Frethem.k (aka w32.frethem.k@mm) is a more robust version of Frethem.e, which uses its own SMTP engine to send e-mail using addresses obtained from infected systems. Mac and Linux users are not affected.
Read the full story An improved CLIE
With the CLIE PEG-T665C, Sony took everything good about the PEG-T615C--including its terrific color screen and solid design--and added a faster processor and MP3 support to make quite an attractive Palm OS PDA. But with Pocket PCs now selling for less than this CLIE and a whole new Palm OS on the horizon, is this the right time to invest in the T665C?
Read the full reviewWrite me at dan.farber@cnet.com

subject: The pandemic masked Canadians' debt problem — what now?
body: First Up Plus, international students facing deportation and cash for turning down your A/C ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ ‌ First Up By Manuela Vega Good morning. Here’s the latest on Canadians’ debt problems, international students facing deportation and Ontario’s new energy conservation initiative. DON’T MISS Canadian Press/Andrew Vaughan economy Canadians are hurting as their debt problems mount — and now the banks are suffering too At the start of the pandemic, interest rates were low, financial support was flowing and banks weren’t aggressively collecting debts. “It was really masking the fact that people were using credit to make ends meet,” one expert said. But now, collections and interest rates are up, he said, and the situation no longer seems manageable. A series of reports this week put it in black and white: Canada has the highest debt-to-GDP ratio in the G7, and credit and car loan delinquencies are increasing. Now, Canada’s Big Five banks have taken a hit, too, as they reported putting aside billions of dollars to protect themselves from bad loans. Christine Dobby reports on the “perfect storm” of debt problems. The aftermath: If banks are earning less, it could eventually become more difficult for Canadians to borrow money, an expert said. Go deeper: An April poll of about 1,500 Canadians found more than half were cutting back on non-essentials to cover their debt, while 43 per cent reported sacrificing on some essentials, and 26 per cent said they would consider taking on extra work. More: With delinquency rates for credit cards and car loans increasing, mortgage defaults could be next, a report warns. Canadian Press File Photo immigration This student can’t blame a consultant for submitting a bogus college admission letter, a tribunal hears Amritraj Singh Batth, 25, is one of several international students from India fighting to stay in Canada after applying for a student visa through the same agent. The uphill battle against deportation comes after their admission letters for post-secondary institutions were found to be bogus. Advocates have said that it’s the students who are victims, finding out only years after the fact that there was an issue with their documentation. But a Canada Border Services Agency told a tribunal Thursday that Batth could not be absolved of his responsibility by blaming an unscrupulous consultant. Nicholas Keung reports on the case and what awaits Batth if the finding is upheld. Context: Some of the international students in the investigation have been in Canada since 2017, completed their studies, received work permits and been on the path to permanent residence. Word from Batth: “If I would have gotten to know prior to coming to Canada, I would never have entered Canada … I never intended to come to Canada illegally. I wanted to have proper legal documentation to come here,” he told the tribunal under cross examination. Dreamstime Photo climate crisis Ontario will give you cash to turn down your A/C Do you have a heat pump or a smart thermostat and central air conditioning? The Ontario government would like to turn down your A/C by a few degrees when the electrical grid is straining under peak demand — and they’ll give you $75 for the right to do it for you, Marco Chown Oved reports. Here’s why Energy Minister Todd Smith says the new program is “a win for customers, a win for the climate and a win for the province.” More: “To be clear: participants are always in control and can opt-out by adjusting their thermostat without losing incentives,” Smith said. Word from the Green Party: The program is “a feeble attempt to slap a bandaid on a crumbling energy system that has worsened thanks to this government’s total mismanagement,” party leader Mike Schreiner said in a statement, adding that “other provinces and countries are moving full-speed ahead with investments in renewable energy.” Wait, what? Shortly after being elected in 2018, Doug Ford’s government cancelled more than 700 renewable energy projects and numerous energy conservation programs after years of industry fleeing the province and electricity demand stagnating. WHAT ELSE David Johnston forgot something important in his report — and it could give the reassurance Canadians need. Do 102 names fit on a ballot?! Here’s everything you need to know about voting in the mayoral election. Mélanie Joly’s office is upset over Conservative MP Leslyn Lewis and controversial preacher Charles McVety’s plan to host an Israeli minister. Mayoral candidates sparred over economic issues during the fourth debate in just two days. Here’s what you need to know. The trip was years in the making. This is what Canada’s Black politicians learned when they visited their U.S. peers. Toronto Pride is mulling cuts to the festival after policing and insurance costs more than tripled. Brampton is the most expensive Ontario city for car insurance. Here’s why the average premium is now over $2,700. The “mom types” trend is silly but irresistible. Which type are you: silky, crunchy, almond or … scrunchie? Did you give your child an old phone to play with? Officials say many of these devices are to blame for accidental 911 calls. Changing a personal brand can be a hard sell, as Elizabeth Holmes’s attempted makeover as a wholesome suburban mom shows. Freedom Mobile has unveiled its first nationwide plan since the Rogers-Shaw deal. From the best Cannes Film Festival in years, the Star’s critic picks the top 10 movies. POV Steve Russell/The Star Three mayoral debates in one day, six long hours and one surprising winner. CLOSE-UP Steve Russell/The Star NATHAN PHILLIPS SQUARE: Six-year-old Canadian Gabriel Duclayna smiles after his parents Kenneth Duclayna and Regine Obsequo are sworn in as Canadian Citizens on Thursday. They are among thirty-seven people from 11 different countries sworn in as new Canadian citizens on Toronto Newcomer Day, an annual event held in May. Thank you for reading. You can reach me and the First Up team at firstup@thestar.ca. Ashley will see you back here tomorrow. subject: Liberty in Our Lifetime: Don't Miss Out – Start Planning Your Trip Today
body:
<<Free Cities Foundation>> [https://libertyinourlifetime.org/]

Dear Subscriber,

Time flies! It's hard to believe, but we are just over 100 days away from the Free Cities Foundation's highly anticipated Liberty in Our Lifetime [https://libertyinourlifetime.org/] conference, taking place from November 1-3. Now is the perfect time to start planning your trip to be part of this unforgettable weekend filled with inspiring talks and quality networking activities in the heart of historic Prague.
[https://xmjhp.mjt.lu/img2/xmjhp/dd5824e2-ef4e-484c-bb54-d88230329b0c/content]
Attendees networking at our 2023 conference
In recent years, more than a dozen zones aspiring to become Free Cities have been established worldwide. These zones are dedicated to protecting individual liberties and fostering a new kind of relationship between citizens and their governments. The Liberty in Our Lifetime conference is the premier annual gathering where representatives of Free City projects from around the globe come together to showcase their achievements and the opportunities they can offer to you and your family.

In addition to featuring important updates from flagship projects such as Ciudad Morazán, Próspera, Montelibero and Sark, this year will also include first-time presentations from new and promising projects in Africa and the Caribbean.
[https://xmjhp.mjt.lu/img2/xmjhp/96134a22-866f-4348-9e1e-12d13a9cc265/content]
Welcome drinks at Liberty in Our Lifetime 2023
Online tickets now available

While we encourage as many of you as possible to join us in Prague, we understand that this may not be possible for everyone. That’s why we also offer online tickets [https://libertyinourlifetime.org/product/liberty-in-our-lifetime-2024-online-ticket/], allowing you to join our conference livestream, ask questions, and receive talk recordings from the comfort of your own home. For more information about our speakers, event details, and to purchase your tickets, please visit our official conference website [https://libertyinourlifetime.org/].

We look forward to seeing you in November.

Best regards,

The Free Cities Foundation Team
With thanks to our conference sponsors:

[https://tipolis.com/]

[https://staatenlos.ch/]

[]
You have received this email because you are subscribed to the Liberty in Our Lifetime newsletter.
